# Earth Opposites

> An interactive web app that lets you discover what's on the exact opposite side of the world (the antipodal point). Click anywhere on a map, drag points on a 3D globe, or browse 120+ curated antipodal city pairs.

## What is an antipodal point?

An antipodal point (or antipode) is the point on Earth's surface that is diametrically opposite to a given location. If you could drill a straight line through the center of the Earth from where you stand, you'd emerge at the antipodal point. Mathematically, if you're at coordinates (lat, lng), your antipode is at (-lat, lng ± 180).

## Features

- **Dual Interactive Maps**: Two OpenStreetMap-powered map panels — one for your chosen point, one for its antipode. Click either map to set a point.
- **3D Globe Visualization**: A d3-geo orthographic projection globe showing Natural Earth land boundaries. Drag to rotate, drag points to reposition them.
- **120+ Antipodal City Pairs**: Curated pairs of real cities that sit (nearly) opposite each other on the globe, such as Madrid ↔ Weber (NZ), Shanghai ↔ Buenos Aires, and Wellington ↔ Salamanca.
- **Real-Time Geocoding**: Automatically resolves coordinates to human-readable place names via Nominatim reverse geocoding.
- **Search & Filter**: Filter the city pair list by name or country.
- **Fun Facts**: Each curated pair includes a fun geographic fact.
- **Random Pair on Load**: Every visit starts with a randomly selected antipodal pair.

## How it works

1. User clicks on either map panel or drags a point on the 3D globe.
2. The app computes the antipodal point using the formula: antipode(lat, lng) = (-lat, lng ± 180).
3. Both maps update to show the selected point and its antipode.
4. Reverse geocoding runs to display place names for both locations.
5. Users can also browse and select from 120+ pre-curated antipodal city pairs.

## Technology

- React 19 with TypeScript
- Vite build tool
- Leaflet for interactive maps
- d3-geo for 3D globe rendering
- world-atlas / TopoJSON for land boundaries
- Nominatim for reverse geocoding

## Data

The curated city pair dataset was built by:
1. Taking a database of world cities with population > 10,000
2. Computing the mathematical antipode for each city
3. Finding the nearest city to each antipode using haversine distance
4. Keeping pairs where the offset distance is < 200 km
5. De-duplicating symmetric pairs and sorting by accuracy

## Source

- Website: https://earth-opposites.vercel.app
- Source code: https://github.com/dokluch/earth_opposites
